# tearDown()

**Instance Method**

Provides an opportunity to perform cleanup after each test method in a test case ends.

## Declaration

```swift
func tearDown()
```

## Discussion

After each test completes, `XCTest` calls [doc://com.apple.xctest/documentation/XCTest/XCTest/tearDown()], then `tearDownWithError()`, followed by [doc://com.apple.xctest/documentation/XCTest/XCTest/tearDown(completion:)]. Override this method to perform any per-test cleanup.

If state cleanup might throw errors, override [doc://com.apple.xctest/documentation/XCTest/XCTest/tearDownWithError()] or [doc://com.apple.xctest/documentation/XCTest/XCTest/tearDown(completion:)] instead. `XCTest` marks the test failed when when it catches errors, or skipped when it catches `XCTSkip`.

## Related Documentation

- **tearDown()**: Provides an opportunity to perform cleanup after a test case ends.

## Setting Up and Tearing Down

- **setUp(completion:)**: Provides an opportunity to reset state asynchronously and handle errors before calling each test method in a test case.
- **setUpWithError()**: Provides an opportunity to reset state and to throw errors before calling each test method in a test case.
- **setUp()**: Provides an opportunity to reset state before calling each test method in a test case.
- **tearDown(completion:)**: Provides an opportunity to perform cleanup asynchronously and handle errors after each test method in a test case ends.
- **tearDownWithError()**: Provides an opportunity to perform cleanup and to throw errors after each test method in a test case ends.
